I mean… were Jokic defending Caruso the way Caruso was defending Jokic, he would have picked up 6 fouls by halftime. Just because a guy is smaller doesn’t mean he should be able to smack the shit out of a bigger guy all game long. Also Jokic was backing him down a bunch back in game 2 but got called for 5 offensive fouls in that game. 1-2 of them were on illegal screens, but at least a few of them were him backing down a smaller guy in the post. I’m not suggesting it’s the reason we lost. But I refuse to believe Alex fucking Caruso is the only guy who can lock Jokic up

The entire lakers 3peat and the 2 championships with kobe/pau was using the triangle against zones?? Same with kerr's championships The triangle works against any and all defenses because it demands perfect/great spacing at all times along with smart and constant movement. The point is that it forces the defense into making tough decisions that the offense knows better than they do

its not that other players weren't good, its that he had maybe 4 or 5 other players to depend on and no depth beyond that. If Jokic himself or one of those playable dudes were cold/off/hurt/etc. then the relative impact on the nuggets was heavier than on OKC, coupled with the fact that OKC has elite players on offense and elite players on defense with a sizable rotation of players (i think there were like 4 or more starting lineups in the regular season for OKC) while SGA is the head of all that.

Fewer assists, more turnovers, worse defense, worse efficiency, worse scoring Had literally his two worst games of the season in games 2 and 3 and only took 9 shots in the close out game. I guess the rebounding was good.

Yes, I have it in the same category as all of those. He averaged around 30/13/10 on 66% True Shooting. To put that into perspective, that's like scoring at the volume Kobe scored at, with the rebounding of Tim Duncan, the assist numbers of Chris Paul, and the efficiency of peak Steph Curry. All in one player. He literally averaged a 30 point triple double. Jokic this season: \- #1 in efficiency (amongst players who average at least 15 a game) \- #2 in assists \- #3 in rebounds \- #3 in points

Dallas doesn't need more defense. They need an offensive PG who's great distributor. Losing one of their few remaining shooters would also be a huge issue. They're already going to have spacing issues but you get rid of Klay and they basically have 0 volume 3 pt shooters and unlike the one team that can kind of make that work (thpugh MPJ is one in the regular season) they don't have a Jokic (was going to say distributor but Jokic is one of one so other drop ins don't really work)

Realest comment. I been saying this same shit. Jokic stats are inflated from playing in this era where there's few dominant big men. He's also a one-way player unlike most of the actual all-time greats. No actual Top 5 center of all time is getting locked up by 6'5" Caruso, but supposed current "best player in the world" and "best offensive center/player of all time" Jokic (7 feet tall, no injuries, in his prime) got locked up in an elimination game? Make it make sense. He's just overhyped because he's the Great White Hope. No other possible explanation why he gets glazed so hard.

Yup. Brook is not completely unplayable in playoffs, Pacers are just an all-time offensive team and the worst matchup for him of anybody in the league. *(He has been exposed even as the prime bucks version of himself before by other playoff teams tho) He can definitely be effective against teams that don’t have a knockdown stretch 5, lightning pace, or wings/guards that can drag him into an iso on the perimeter and then blow by for easy paint touches. The better your perimeter/poa defense is, the more playable + effective Brook will be. Would be a great pickup for Warriors.

I never said he was better than MJ and Bron. However, for offense only, there's an argument. Great rebounder, elite efficiency, can score from anywhere, and an elite passer. If Jokic was also an elite defender, he'd be in the goat convo versus just the top 10 all time convo.

It’s not about the individual matchup, it’s about the team defense. Caruso couldn’t guard Jokic 1v1, but he can be effective as the primary defender if Chet is there to help. And Randle is good, but Caruso, JDub, Dort, Hartenstein, JWill, even Cason in bursts will be able to matchup with him, depending on the scheme
